overview: With only a single component and no inter-service dependencies, the entire system is implemented in one phase. A Bottom-Up strategy is used by default — the sole web application is self-contained, requiring no external services, databases, or APIs. This means the full implementation can be built, tested, and deployed in a single pass.

phases.name: Foundation & Delivery
phases.description: The single hello-world-web component has no dependencies on any other service, so it forms the entire implementation. This phase delivers the complete working system: a Go net/http server that serves the Hello World page, a /health endpoint, and a catch-all 404 handler — all containerised and ready for deployment.

components.name: hello-world-web
responsibilities: Serve a pre-rendered 'Hello, World!' HTML page at the root path (/) with HTTP 200 and correct Content-Type.
Expose a /health liveness/readiness endpoint returning HTTP 200 JSON {"status":"ok"} at all times.
Return a user-friendly HTML 404 page for any undefined route, including a link back to /.
Apply security response headers on every request (suppressed Server header, CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.) via middleware.
Handle graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM with a 10-second drain timeout and log each request's method, path, status, and duration.
components.architectureContext: This component is the sole service in the architecture and the only entry point for all user traffic. The platform ingress layer terminates HTTPS and forwards plain HTTP to the container on port 9090. There are no upstream services, databases, or external APIs to call — all responses are served from in-memory string constants. The /health route is consumed exclusively by the platform's liveness and readiness probes.
keyConsiderations: Use http.NewServeMux() with an exact-match route for '/' to prevent the default subtree-matching behaviour from swallowing all paths into the index handler — any path other than '/' and '/health' must reach the catch-all 404 handler.

All HTML responses must be served from pre-allocated in-memory string constants (indexHTML, notFoundHTML) — no template execution at request time — to guarantee sub-millisecond handler latency and meet the 500 ms response-time SLA under 100 concurrent users.

The multi-stage Dockerfile (golang:1.22-alpine builder → scratch runtime) produces a minimal binary image; CGO must be disabled (CGO_ENABLED=0) so the statically linked binary runs correctly inside a scratch container with no libc.
The Server response header must be explicitly set to an empty string in the middleware wrapper to override Go's default 'Go' value, satisfying the requirement to suppress server version disclosure.

Graceful shutdown must capture both SIGINT and SIGTERM via os/signal and call server.Shutdown() with a context deadline of 10 seconds to allow in-flight requests to complete before the process exits, supporting zero-downtime rolling restarts.
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web App
  description: Serves the Hello World HTML page and health endpoint.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns the main Hello World HTML page.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page with Hello World greeting.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns 200 OK when the service is healthy.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all 404
      description: Returns a user-friendly 404 Not Found page for undefined routes.
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Page not found.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
